sn24.4:1.1: At Sāvatthī. "Bhikkhus, when what exists, by clinging to what, by adhering to what, does such a view as this arise: ‘I might not be, and it might not be for me; I will not be,andit will not be for me′?"
sn24.4:1.2: "Venerable sir, our teachings are rooted in the Blessed One … ."
sn24.4:4.1: "When, bhikkhus, a noble disciple has abandoned perplexity in these six cases … he is then called a noble disciple who is a stream-enterer … with enlightenment as his destination."
